_comment: Canonical Tribunal model. The caller (Sage or Dash) never proposes shell commands; the caller sends the Tribunal a natural-language `request` plus optional `guidelines`, and the Tribunal is the sole authority for producing the final command string. There is no fallback — when the Tribunal fails, the tool call fails.
name: Tribunal
description: Command authority. Converts the caller's natural-language request + guidelines into a precise shell command through multi-perspective voting and verification.

reasoning.description: The logic behind the member's verdict or revision.

command.description: The proposed command string for this member.

request.description: Caller's natural-language description of what the Operator must accomplish. No shell syntax.

guidelines.description: Optional constraints on the command itself (flags/tools to prefer or avoid, portability, edge cases the command must handle, whitelist/blacklist restrictions). Plain language, never shell syntax. Guidelines describe the command, not its output.

vote_winner.description: The command that won the weighted majority vote.

outcome.description: Terminal outcome of the pipeline. Only successful outcomes are enumerated; Tribunal failures raise typed TribunalError subclasses instead of producing a fallback result.

vote_breakdown.description: Full uniform-vote breakdown with member attribution, dissent clusters, consensus strength, and optional tie-break record. Present on every non-failed Tribunal result.

candidates_by_command.description: normalised command -> list of member_ids that produced it

winner.description: Normalised command that won; null on consensus_failed

dissenters_by_command.description: losing command -> list of member_ids that produced it

tie_broken.description: Whether the winner required tie-break resolution
